meaning: adj.反应的,起反作用的,反动的,电抗性的
examples: I want our organization to be less reactive and more proactive.
The police presented a reactive rather than preventive strategy against crime.
The cold-bed batch type steam dyeing process for woolen yarn by reactive dyes is described in this book.
Strategery represented a flimsy veneer of forethought over a largely reactive and often ineffective game-plan.
In turn, that can cut C-reactive protein, a marker for inflammation, by up to 40%.
